titleStmt.title: Telegrams from Andrew Philip Magill to Henry, 4 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Andrew Philip Magill

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Two telegrams from Andrew Philip Magill (1871–1941), private secretary to Henry Edward Duke (1855–1939), Chief Secretary for Ireland, to a Henry, perhaps Henry Duke. In the earlier telegram Magill asks for the date of an article which was published in the Irish Independent before the execution of James Connolly (1868-1916). The article urged the leaders of the Rising should not be shown any clemency. Magill’s telegram later the same day indicates that he has got the information he required.James Connolly , was one of the leaders of the Easter Rising in 1916. He was badly wounded during the fighting at the G.P.O. and he was executed by firing squad in Kilmainham Gaol on 12 May 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5620/13486

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
From Magill To Henry My wire I have now got Independent of
May 10th I think leading article in that
copy is the one referred to so don't trouble further A /P M 5/8/16
From Magill To Henry Can you give me date of Article in Independent
issued before execution of Connolly in which it was
urged that the leaders in rebellion should not be
shown any clemency I reply:-
Henry & Librarian gone. Will get wire
fordate tomorrow. Registrar
II Mrs Henry
